data.title: International media test drives GWM Hi4 technology at Dujiangyan, China. GWM's Hi4 hybrid 4WD system impresses with 4WD performance at 2WD consumption level. GWM WEY G9 and GWM TANK 500 Hi4-T receive positive feedback.
data.description: In Baoding, China, an innovative development in driving technology has been introduced by GWM. The Hi4 intelligent hybrid 4WD system by GWM is revolutionizing the driving experience by providing the performance of a 4WD vehicle while maintaining the fuel efficiency of a 2WD vehicle. Through test drives of the GWM WEY G9 and GWM TANK 500 Hi4-T models in various conditions such as city traffic, mountainous terrains, and wet unpaved tracks, international media outlets have witnessed the exceptional efficiency and performance of the GWM Hi4 system. This new technology is receiving positive reviews for its ability to enhance driving capabilities across different environments.

data.title: Sun's wallet blacklisted after $9 million in WLFI tokens moved; Sun denies selling, says only testing occurred.
data.description: Justin Sun, the founder of Tron (CRYPTO: TRX) and a prominent cryptocurrency billionaire, had a wallet address associated with him blacklisted after $9 million worth of World Liberty Financial (WLFI) tokens were transferred out of the address. The on-chain analytics firm Arkham disclosed this information along with evidence from Etherscan showing that World Liberty Financial had blocked the address from making further token transfers. The address linked to Sun moved 50 million WLFI tokens, amounting to $9.11 million based on current prices, to an undisclosed destination. Following these transactions, Sun clarified on X that the address had conducted "general exchange deposit tests" with small amounts before dispersing the tokens to various addresses, emphasizing that no buying or selling activities had taken place. This incident had prompted the address to be restricted. For more details, you can find the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Salesforce to pay $0.416 per share quarterly dividend on Oct 9, 2025, for shareholders recorded by Sep 17, 2025.
data.description: In San Francisco, Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the top AI CRM globally, has disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.416 per share. The dividend will be distributed on October 9, 2025, to shareholders listed on September 17, 2025. Salesforce aids businesses of all scales in reimagining their operations with AI. Agentforce, the initial digital labor tool for corporations, effortlessly links with Customer 360 applications, Data Cloud, and Ein.

data.title: Gala Games upgrades referral program from 2 to 5 levels, allowing users to earn more rewards with larger networks.
data.description: Gala Games has enhanced their referral system, now offering users the opportunity to earn rewards through five friend levels instead of the previous two. This change allows users to increase their earnings as they expand their network.

data.title: Trump hurries trade deals while US firms abroad remain uncertain.
data.description: President Donald Trump is working quickly to complete new trade deals before the month's end. Meanwhile, American companies that operate overseas are still seeking clarity on current agreements.
